checks.oneListOnly: You should only create one list. Make sure you only use [] once.
checks.usePush: You should use push() to add items to your list.

packALunch.name: Pack a Lunch
packALunch.description: Write a function that packs a lunchbox with a sandwich, drink, and snack. If the drink is a milkshake, leave it out of the lunchbox because it's too big.

regularLunch.name: Regular lunch
regularLunch.description: A normal lunch with water - everything fits in the lunchbox.

milkshakeLunch.name: Milkshake lunch
milkshakeLunch.description: The milkshake is too big for the lunchbox, so it gets left out.

anotherRegular.name: Another regular lunch
anotherRegular.description: Another normal lunch where everything fits.

anotherMilkshake.name: Another milkshake lunch
anotherMilkshake.description: Another lunch with a milkshake that doesn't fit.

basicShape.question: What's the basic shape of my answer?
basicShape.answer: A list. Start with an empty list and add the allowed items to it one at a time.

addItems.question: How do I add items to a list?
addItems.answer: Use `push()`. It takes the list and the item, and gives back the new (longer) list.

leaveMilkshakeOut.question: How do I leave the milkshake out?
leaveMilkshakeOut.answer: Use an if-statement. Only push the drink if it's not equal to `"milkshake"`.

listNotGrowing.question: Why isn't my list growing?
listNotGrowing.answer: `push()` doesn't modify the list in place. It returns the new list. You need to reassign, for example `lunchbox = lunchbox.push(item)` (or the equivalent in your language).

push.description: Returns a new list with the element added to the end (provided by level stdlib)
